scripts.title: Never Paid Myself
scripts.hook: I've built a quarter-million dollar business and I've never paid myself a single dollar.

scripts.title: 2 Seconds to Hook
scripts.hook: Your hook has 2 seconds to work. And yours isn't working. Here's why.

scripts.title: The Math on Content
scripts.hook: How much is one new customer worth to your business?

scripts.title: Fire Your ChatGPT Agency
scripts.hook: If your social media agency is using ChatGPT to write all your scripts — fire them. Today.

scripts.title: Script This: Plumber
scripts.hook: Someone told me plumbing is the most boring business to make content for.

scripts.title: Script This: Med Spa
scripts.hook: Med spas spend $5,000 a month on ads. I'm about to write them a script for free that'll outperform all of it.
scripts.topic: Showing how to script fear-based med spa content that sells without selling — the client story format.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Med spas spend $5,000 a month on ads. I'm about to write them a script for free that'll outperform all of it.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what every med spa gets wrong with their content. They post before-and-afters with zero context. No story, no emotion, just a side-by-side pic and 'book now.' That doesn't make anyone feel anything.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's what actually works—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'She almost canceled her appointment because she was terrified of looking fake.'

Body: 'This is Sarah. She wanted Botox for a year but kept scrolling horror stories online. Frozen faces. People who don't look like themselves anymore. So she almost didn't come in. But here's what we told her — good Botox doesn't make you look different. It makes you look like you slept 8 hours. Watch her reaction seeing herself for the first time.'

Then you show the reveal. That's it. That's the whole video.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not selling Botox. You're selling the relief that it won't ruin your face. Every med spa client has that fear. Address the fear, show the outcome, let the emotion do the selling.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you run a med spa and your content isn't doing this — your content isn't working. Drop your business in the comments, I'll tell you what to post.

scripts.value: Reveals the psychology behind med spa buying decisions — fear vs. desire. Shows the exact content format that works.
scripts.cta: What treatment would YOU want to see scripted? 👇
scripts.caption:
Med spas are spending $5K/month on ads.

I wrote them a script for free that'll outperform all of it.

Most med spa content is before/afters with zero emotion.
No story. Just a photo and 'book now.'

That doesn't sell.

Here's what does:

Address the fear.
Show the outcome.
Let the emotion close it.

Drop your business below — I'll tell you what to post. 👇

visuals.text: Open confident, slight smirk — you're about to prove a point
When delivering the embedded script, shift energy — become the med spa's voice
Text overlay: '$5,000 ads vs. free script' at hook for visual contrast

scripts.title: Script This: Attorney
scripts.hook: Lawyers think they can't go viral. I wrote a personal injury attorney a script in under a minute that would get more leads than a billboard.
scripts.topic: Scripting a PI attorney using the 'expose the threat' format — building trust by giving away insider knowledge.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Lawyers think they can't go viral. I wrote a personal injury attorney a script in under a minute that would get more leads than a billboard.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Every PI attorney's content is the same. Guy in a suit pointing at text on screen: 'Were you injured in a car accident? Call us today.' Nobody stops for that. You know why? Because it sounds like every other lawyer. And people don't trust lawyers — that's the real problem you need to solve first.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: So here's the script—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'The insurance company is hoping you don't watch this video.'

Body: 'If you just got in a car accident, the insurance adjuster is going to call you within 48 hours. They're going to sound really nice. They're going to ask how you're feeling. And then they're going to offer you a check. Do NOT take it. That first offer? It's designed to be as low as possible before you realize how bad your injuries actually are. Here's exactly what to say when they call—'

Then you give them the script. Word for word. Free.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not saying 'hire me.' You're giving them something they desperately need in a moment of panic. That builds more trust than any billboard ever could. And when they DO need a lawyer? You're the one who helped them when nobody else did.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're an attorney and your content is just you pointing at text — we need to talk. Drop your practice area below and I'll script it.

scripts.value: Shows how attorneys can build trust through radical transparency — giving away insider knowledge builds authority faster than any ad.
scripts.cta: What type of lawyer should I script next? ⚖️👇
scripts.caption:
Lawyers think they can't go viral.

I wrote a personal injury attorney a script that gets more leads than a billboard.

Most attorney content: guy in a suit, pointing at text, 'call us today.'

Nobody stops for that.

Here's what actually works:

Give people something they desperately need — before they need you.

That's the content strategy.

Drop your practice area below — I'll script it. ⚖️

visuals.text: Direct, intense energy at camera — you're making a bold claim
When delivering the embedded script, shift energy to match the urgency of the warning
Text overlay: 'More leads than a billboard' at hook

scripts.title: The Script Template
scripts.hook: I'm about to give you the exact script template I use for every single client. Screenshot this.
scripts.topic: Giving away the 5-line fill-in-the-blank script template — the highest save-bait content possible.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: I'm about to give you the exact script template I use for every single client. Screenshot this.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Every viral business video follows the same structure. It doesn't matter if you're a dentist, a roofer, or a fitness coach. The framework is the same. And most people overcomplicate it.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the template. Ready?

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Line one — the hook: '[Your customer's biggest fear] is costing you [dollar amount or consequence].'

Line two — the problem: 'Most [type of business] try to fix this by [common wrong approach]. But that actually makes it worse because [reason].'

Line three — the solution: 'Here's what actually works: [your unique approach in one sentence].'

Line four — the proof: 'We did this for [client type] and [specific result with a number].'

Line five — the CTA: 'If you want the same thing, [simple next step].'

That's it. Five lines. Fill in the blanks. You have a script.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: This template works because it follows how people actually make decisions. Fear, problem, solution, proof, action. In that order. Don't rearrange it. Don't add fluff. Just fill in the blanks and hit record.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Save this. Use it. If it works — and it will — come back and tell me.

scripts.value: Gives away the actual tool — the fill-in-the-blank script framework used for every CMP client. Highest save potential of any content we've made.
scripts.cta: Save 🔖 + tag someone who needs this
scripts.caption:
The exact script template I use for every client.

Fill in the blanks. Hit record.

Line 1 — Hook: [Fear] is costing you [consequence]
Line 2 — Problem: Most [biz type] try [wrong approach]...
Line 3 — Solution: Here's what actually works: [your method]
Line 4 — Proof: We did this for [client] and [result]
Line 5 — CTA: If you want this, [next step]

That's it. 5 lines.

Save this and use it this week. 🔖

visuals.text: NEEDS text overlays — each line appears on screen as he says it. This is the most important visual element.
Generous, teaching energy — you're literally giving away the playbook
End with 'Save this' while pointing at screen — direct prompt to save

scripts.title: Why Your Content Feels Off
scripts.hook: You're posting consistently, getting decent views, but no one's reaching out. Here's why.
scripts.topic: Diagnosing the most common content strategy mistake — making content about yourself instead of your customer's fear.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: You're posting consistently, getting decent views, but no one's reaching out. Here's why.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Your content is entertaining but it doesn't position you as the solution to a problem. You've got attention but no direction. People watch, they like, they move on. They never think 'I should hire this person.'

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: And it comes down to one missing piece—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Every piece of content needs to answer one question for the viewer: 'What do I do next?' Not with a 'link in bio' or 'DM me.' With value so specific that they think 'this person clearly knows my problem better than I do.'

When a roofer posts 'We do great roofs!' that's nice. When a roofer posts 'Your roof is leaking in a spot you can't see — here's how to check in 30 seconds' — now they're the expert. Now there's a reason to call THEM.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop making content about what you do. Start making content about what your customer is afraid of. That's the shift.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're posting and not getting leads — the content isn't broken. The strategy is.

scripts.value: Diagnoses the exact frustration most business owners feel — posting without results. Names the real cause and gives the fix in one sentence.
scripts.cta: What's your business? I'll tell you what to post 👇
scripts.caption:
You're posting. Getting views. Nobody's reaching out.

Here's the actual problem:

Your content is entertaining. It's not positioning.

People watch, they like, they scroll.
They never think 'I need to hire this person.'

The fix:

Stop making content about what YOU do.
Start making content about what your customer is AFRAID of.

That one shift changes everything.

What's your business? Tell me below — I'll tell you what to post. 👇

visuals.text: Calm, diagnostic energy — you're identifying a problem they've felt but couldn't name
Text overlay on roofer example: 'We do great roofs' ❌ vs. 'Leak you can't see' ✅
End with the principle on screen: 'Content about THEIR fear → not YOUR service'

scripts.title: I'll Script Your Business in 60 Seconds
scripts.hook: Comment your business and I'll write you a viral script in 60 seconds. Let's go.
scripts.topic: Interactive challenge — script a business live on camera using the CMP framework. Drives comment engagement.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Comment your business and I'll write you a viral script in 60 seconds. Let's go.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text:
Alright — someone said they're a personal trainer. Let's go.

Most fitness content is 'here's a workout.' Cool. Everyone does that. Nobody hires a trainer because of a workout video. They hire because they're STUCK.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the script—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'You've been working out for 6 months and you look exactly the same. Here's what's actually wrong.'

Body: 'It's not your effort. You're showing up, you're sweating, you're sore the next day. The problem is your program has no structure. You're doing random workouts from Instagram and expecting a specific result. That's like driving without a GPS and wondering why you're lost. Here's the 3 things you need to fix this week—'

Then you give three specific, actionable tips. Not generic. Specific.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: See what I did? I didn't start with the solution. I started with the frustration. Because that's what makes someone stop scrolling. They see their own problem reflected back at them. THEN they're ready to hear the answer.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Drop your business in the comments. I'll script the next one live.

scripts.value: Interactive format that drives massive comment engagement — turns viewers into participants and proves scripting ability live.
scripts.cta: Drop your business 👇 I'll script it
scripts.caption:
Comment your business — I'll write you a viral script in 60 seconds.

I picked: Personal Trainer.

Fitness content mistake: showing workouts.
Nobody hires a trainer for a workout video.
They hire because they're STUCK.

So you start with the frustration.
Not the solution.

That's the shift that changes everything.

Drop your business below — I'll script the next one. 👇

visuals.text: Playful, fast energy — this is a challenge, not a lecture
Pretend to read a comment on phone before scripting — adds authenticity
Optional: countdown timer graphic showing '60 seconds' at the start

scripts.title: The Vanity Metric Trap
scripts.hook: If your content is getting 10,000 views and you still have no clients — it's not the algorithm. You're measuring the wrong thing.
scripts.topic: Exposing vanity metrics (views) vs. conversion metrics (saves, shares) — the real scorecard for business content.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: If your content is getting 10,000 views and you still have no clients — it's not the algorithm. You're measuring the wrong thing.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You're watching views go up. Followers ticking. Telling yourself it's working. But your DMs are empty. Your phone isn't ringing. You're measuring vanity metrics. And vanity metrics pay nobody's bills.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing most content coaches will never say to your face...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Views are the audience's glance. Saves are their receipt. Shares are their referral. Those three — saves, shares, comments asking questions — that's your actual conversion funnel.

A post with 200 views and 4 saves will get you more clients than a post with 10K views and zero saves. I'd put money on it. Because saves mean: I need this. Shares mean: my friend needs this too. That's buyer behavior.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop asking how many people saw it. Start asking: did it make someone stop, save it, and think 'I need this person?' That is the metric that matters.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're posting content without tracking saves and shares — start today. That's your real scorecard. Everything else is noise.

scripts.value: Reframes how service business owners measure content success — shifts focus from vanity metrics to conversion indicators.
scripts.cta: Track saves. Not views.
scripts.caption:
Stop chasing views. Start tracking what actually drives clients. 📊

If your content isn't getting saves and shares, it's not converting — no matter what the view count says.

Views = glance
Saves = receipt
Shares = referral

That's your real funnel.

Drop a 🔖 if you needed to hear this.

visuals.text: Shota tight to camera, eye contact. Text overlay at hook: 'Views ≠ Clients.'
Mid-script: quick B-roll cut to analytics screen showing high views / low saves
Return to Shota for close. Clean, sharp cuts. No music until outro.

scripts.title: 200 Followers, More Clients Than a 20K Account
scripts.hook: I know creators with 20,000 followers who can't get a client. And a business with 200 followers that's fully booked. Here's the difference.
scripts.topic: Busting the follower count myth — targeted, specific content beats broad audiences every time.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: I know creators with 20,000 followers who can't get a client. And a business with 200 followers that's fully booked. Here's the difference.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: We've been sold a lie. That growth equals revenue. That follower count is the scoreboard. So business owners spend months — sometimes years — chasing a number. While their competitors with a fraction of the following are booking clients every single week.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: Followers are vanity. Trust is currency. The 200-follower account? Every post speaks to one person with one problem. The audience is small — but it's the right audience. The 20K account? Broad. Generic. Built for the algorithm, not the buyer. There's a difference between a big crowd and the right room. You don't need to go viral. You need to be undeniable to the right 200 people.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Build content for your buyer. Not for the algorithm. Because the algorithm doesn't pay your rent. Your clients do.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you run a business — your goal is never 'go viral.' Your goal is 'book clients.' Those are completely different content strategies. And I build the second one.

scripts.value: Gives permission to stop chasing follower count. Reframes the goal from virality to specificity. Massive relief factor for small business owners.
scripts.cta: Build for your buyer. Not the algorithm.
scripts.caption:
I've watched businesses with 200 followers outperform accounts with 20K.

Not because of luck. Because they understood one thing:

Your follower count is not your scorecard. Your booked calendar is. 💼

What's your actual goal with content right now? Drop it below — I read every comment.

visuals.text: Shota walking and talking — movement adds energy and authenticity (BiP feel)
Optional: quick cut to phone showing a small follower count vs. a full booking calendar
Candid, no teleprompter energy. Natural lighting preferred.

scripts.title: Posting Consistently Is the Worst Advice
scripts.hook: Posting consistently is the worst advice anyone ever gave a business owner. And every marketing guru says it.
scripts.topic: Hot take — quality over quantity. One extraordinary post beats 30 mediocre ones.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Posting consistently is the worst advice anyone ever gave a business owner. And every marketing guru says it.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Post every day. Stay consistent. Show up even when you don't feel like it. You've heard it. Most people follow it. And most people are posting garbage on a schedule — wondering why nothing's working. Consistency without strategy is just noise with a calendar.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: One extraordinary post will outperform thirty mediocre ones. Every single time. One video that stops the scroll — says something nobody else has said — speaks directly to your buyer — that's worth more than a month of daily posts. The algorithm doesn't reward frequency. It rewards engagement. And engagement follows quality, not quantity.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Don't post more. Post better. Even if that means once a week. Make that one post so good it earns saves. Shares. DMs. Clients. Because garbage on a schedule is still garbage.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: So before you stress about posting every day — ask yourself one question: Is my content actually good?

scripts.value: Gives exhausted business owners permission to slow down and focus on quality. Validates what they already suspect — frequency isn't the answer.
scripts.cta: Post better. Not more.
scripts.caption:
Hot take: 'Post consistently' has ruined more small businesses than it's helped.

One great post > 30 average ones.

Fight me in the comments. ⬇️

visuals.text: Shota slightly leaned in toward camera — confrontational, confident energy
Bold graphic text overlay at hook. Fast, punchy cuts for body.
Text callout on screen: 'Consistency without strategy = noise.'

scripts.title: The One-Platform Rule
scripts.hook: Stop trying to be on every platform. There is one that's right for your business. Here's how to know which one.
scripts.topic: Depth over breadth — mastering one platform beats mediocrity on five.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Stop trying to be on every platform. There is one that's right for your business. Here's how to know which one.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You're posting on TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and YouTube. Mediocre results on all of them. Because you're spread too thin. Your content is diluted. Your energy is split. You're playing five games at 50% instead of one game at 100%.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: Pick the platform where your buyer already is. B2B services? LinkedIn. Consumer-facing brand? Instagram or TikTok. Education and tutorials? YouTube. Find out where your ideal client is scrolling at 10pm. Go there. Master it. Own it. One platform, completely dominated, will get you more clients than five platforms you half-ass.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The goal is not to be everywhere. The goal is to be undeniable somewhere. Depth beats breadth. Always has. Always will.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you want help finding your platform and building a content system around it — that's exactly what I do. Link in bio.

scripts.value: Gives clarity and permission to focus. Removes overwhelm. Provides a simple framework to pick the right platform.
scripts.cta: One platform. 100%.
scripts.caption:
You don't need to be on every platform. You need to own one. 🎯

Which platform is your main focus right now? Drop it below — I'll tell you if it's the right call for your business.

visuals.text: Text overlay cycles through platform logos: TikTok → Instagram → Facebook → LinkedIn → YouTube → then holds on a single arrow: '→ ONE.'
Shota at desk, authoritative setup. Calm, clear delivery.
Clean, minimal. Feels like a revelation.

scripts.title: Script This: Real Estate Agent
scripts.hook: Realtors are spending thousands on Zillow leads. I'm about to write one a script that brings them in for free.
scripts.topic: Scripting a realtor using the 'expose the secret' format — giving buyers insider negotiation knowledge.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Realtors are spending thousands on Zillow leads. I'm about to write one a script that brings them in for free.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Real estate agents are some of the most over-marketed, under-differentiated professionals in any city. They all post the same thing — listing photos, sold signs, and market updates nobody asked for. Meanwhile, their buyers and sellers are on Instagram asking questions that nobody is answering.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Here's the script. Hook: 'Your real estate agent is lying to you — and it's costing you $30,000.'

Body: 'Most buyers don't negotiate because their agent doesn't want the deal to fall through. So they push you to accept the first counter. I'm going to tell you exactly how to negotiate closing costs, inspection credits, and the final price — so you keep that money in your pocket.'

CTA: 'Comment NEGOTIATE and I'll send you my buyer negotiation script.'

That post gets saved. That post gets shared. That post books showings. Not a Zillow ad. A 60-second video.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: That's what content can do when it gives people something they actually needed to know. Not a sold sign. Not a market update. A secret they didn't know they were missing.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're a realtor and you want a full content system built around your market — link's in bio. And comment your industry below — you might be next.

scripts.value: Shows realtors (and their potential clients) how expert knowledge + content beats paid ads. Demonstrates the 'expose the secret' format in real estate.
scripts.cta: Content that earns trust → books clients.
scripts.caption:
Real estate agents: stop posting sold signs. Start posting answers to the questions your buyers are Googling at 11pm. 🏠

Which industry do you want me to script next? Drop it below — I read every one.

visuals.text: Shota delivers the embedded script in character — becomes the realtor's voice, then snaps back to himself
The embedded script text appears on screen as he speaks it
Fast cuts during the embedded script. Return to calm, confident Shota for outro.

scripts.title: The AI Content Test
scripts.hook: There's a 3-second test that instantly tells me if content was written by AI. Most business owners are failing it right now.
scripts.topic: Exposing AI-generated content patterns vs. authentic human voice — a simple 3-second test any business owner can run on their own posts.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: There's a 3-second test that instantly tells me if content was written by AI. Most business owners are failing it right now.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: The test is simple. Read the first sentence of your last post out loud. Does it sound like something you'd actually say to a client sitting across from you? Or does it sound like a LinkedIn post from 2019? AI writes in patterns. It uses phrases like 'in today's fast-paced world' and 'it's important to note that' and 'leverage your unique value proposition.' None of that sounds human. All of it kills trust instantly.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: The problem isn't that AI writes bad content. The problem is AI writes content that sounds like every other business in your category. And when you sound like everyone else — you disappear. Your actual speaking voice is the differentiator. Quick fix: Record yourself answering one client question out loud. Transcribe it. That's your script. Messy, human, real. That's what works.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The businesses winning right now aren't the ones with the biggest budgets. They're the ones who sound like a person. That's it. That's the whole game.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Run your last 3 posts through this test right now. Read them out loud. If you'd never say it to a client's face — rewrite it. If you want help building a content voice that actually sounds like you? That's exactly what we do at CMP. Link's in bio.

scripts.value: Gives business owners a tangible, instant diagnostic for their AI-generated content problem. Positions authentic voice as the competitive advantage. Drives saves + shares from people who recognize the problem.
scripts.cta: Write how you TALK. Not how AI thinks you sound.
scripts.caption:
There's a 3-second test that tells me if content was written by AI.

Most businesses are failing it.

The test: read your last post out loud.
Does it sound like you talking to a client?
Or does it sound like a LinkedIn post from 2019?

AI writes in patterns.
'In today's fast-paced world...'
'Leverage your unique value proposition...'
'It's important to note that...'

None of that sounds human.
All of it kills trust.

Fix: record yourself answering a client question out loud.
Transcribe it.
That's your script.

Run your last 3 posts through this test right now. 👇

visuals.text: Open close to camera, confident — you've spotted something most people miss
Text overlay at hook: '3-second AI test' — let it land before explaining
When listing AI phrases, deliver in slightly flat/robotic tone to contrast with your natural voice
End with the fix on screen: 'Record → Transcribe → Post'

scripts.title: Going Viral Is the Most Expensive Way to Get Zero Clients
scripts.hook: Going viral is the most expensive way to get zero clients. I'm done pretending otherwise.
scripts.topic: Contrarian take: chasing virality is the wrong goal for service businesses — trust-building content for a targeted audience beats broad reach every time.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Going viral is the most expensive way to get zero clients. I'm done pretending otherwise.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Everyone's chasing reach. Big numbers. Watch counts. And I get it — a viral video feels like winning. For a day. But viral content is built to talk to everyone. Which means it talks to no one who will actually hire you. The local business owner with 500 followers who posts specific, targeted content about their customer's exact problem? Booking clients every week. The guy with a viral post and 10,000 new followers? Not one of them is in his market.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: Viral is built for audiences. Clients are built on trust. Those are two completely different content strategies. Trust content says: 'I understand your exact problem. I've solved it before. Here's proof.' Viral content says: 'Look at me.' One of those sentences leads to a sales conversation. The other gets a like and a scroll.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop optimizing for reach. Start optimizing for the right 50 people seeing your post and thinking — 'I need to call this person.' That's the only metric that pays your rent.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: The goal isn't to go viral. The goal is to be unavoidable to the people who need what you do. One is ego. The other is a business. Drop a comment if you've been chasing the wrong number.

scripts.value: Gives exhausted, confused business owners permission to stop chasing vanity. Reframes the entire content goal. Highly shareable — business owners will send this to their partners, marketers, and social media managers.
scripts.cta: Relevant > Viral. Every time.
scripts.caption:
Hot take: going viral is the most expensive way to get zero clients.

Viral content talks to everyone.
Which means it talks to no one who will actually hire you.

Trust content says:
'I understand your problem. I've solved it. Here's proof.'

Viral content says:
'Look at me.'

One leads to a sales call.
The other gets a like and a scroll.

Stop optimizing for reach.
Start optimizing for the right 50 people thinking 'I need to call this person.'

Fight me in the comments. ⬇️

visuals.text: Shota slightly leaned in — calm, not angry, just done with the narrative
Text overlay at hook: 'Going viral = zero clients?' — let the question hang
Fast cuts in BODY when listing what each content type 'says' — punch the contrast
Call for comments explicitly in outro — this hot take will generate debate

scripts.title: The First Client I Landed With Content
scripts.hook: The first client I ever landed from Instagram content was from a video I almost didn't post.
scripts.topic: First-person story of landing the first paying client through organic content — proving the system works, giving permission to post the 'obvious' thing.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: The first client I ever landed from Instagram content was from a video I almost didn't post.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: It was a reel I filmed on my phone in my apartment. No set. No production. Just me talking about why most service businesses sound exactly the same on social media — and why that's quietly killing their leads. I almost didn't post it. I thought it was too basic. Too obvious. Like — who doesn't already know this?

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: I posted it anyway. Three days later, I got a DM from a business owner in Detroit. He said: 'You described my exact problem word for word. I didn't even know that was why my content wasn't working until I watched your video.' We had a call that week. He signed. And that taught me the most important lesson I know about content: obvious to you is not obvious to them. The stuff you think everyone already knows? That's exactly what someone is desperate to hear explained out loud.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The post doesn't need to be complex. It doesn't need high production. It just needs to be true. Specific. And spoken directly at the one person who needed to hear it today.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: That first client? He's still a client. And it started with a video I almost deleted. If you've got something sitting in your drafts that you're second-guessing — post it. Someone needs it more than you think.

scripts.value: Emotional proof-of-concept story that gives permission to post imperfect content. Shows the exact mechanism (DM → call → close) that content can drive. Relief factor: you don't need a polished video to land a client.
scripts.cta: Post the 'obvious' thing. Someone needs it.
scripts.caption:
The first client I landed from Instagram was from a video I almost didn't post.

I filmed it on my phone.
No set. No production.
Just me talking about why most service businesses sound exactly the same — and why it's hurting their leads.

I thought it was too basic. Too obvious.

Then I got a DM from a business owner in Detroit:
'You described my exact problem word for word.'

We had a call that week. He signed. He's still a client.

Lesson:
Obvious to you ≠ obvious to them.

The thing you think everyone already knows?
Someone out there desperately needs to hear you say it.

Post the obvious thing. 📤

visuals.text: Walking-and-talking OR seated with natural lighting — Build in Public, not polished
Consider showing phone screen briefly while describing filming in apartment
When delivering client DM quote, slow down — that's the emotional core
Keep cuts natural, not rapid — this is a story, not a list

scripts.title: Script This: HVAC
scripts.hook: Someone told me HVAC is the most boring business to make content about. I wrote them a script in 60 seconds that got more leads than their Google Ads.
scripts.topic: Scripting a trades/HVAC business using the 'expose the threat before it's too late' format — homeowner fear-based content that earns DMs over Google Ads.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Someone told me HVAC is the most boring business to make content about. I wrote them a script in 60 seconds that got more leads than their Google Ads.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what most HVAC companies post. Before and after of a unit they replaced. 'Serving the Detroit metro area since 1994.' A promo for 10% off this weekend. Nobody stops for that. Nobody shares that. Nobody calls because of that. The problem isn't HVAC content. The problem is they're posting the business instead of the customer's fear.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the script I wrote them—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'Your AC is going to fail this summer. Here's how to know before it costs you $4,000.'

Body: 'There are five signs your AC unit is about to die — and most homeowners miss all of them until it's 95 degrees outside and they're calling an emergency line at 11pm. Sign one: your energy bill went up 20% or more compared to last year. That means the unit is working twice as hard to do half the job. Sign two: you hear a clicking noise when it first kicks on. That's a dying capacitor. $150 to replace now — $3,500 to replace the whole unit when it fails. I'll show you the other three—'

Then you cut. They DM you. Every time.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not making HVAC content. You're making content about a homeowner's worst summer nightmare. Address that fear. Then position yourself as the person who could've prevented it. Any business can do this. You just have to find the fear first.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Drop your business below. I'll tell you the fear your customer has that you should be scripting to. And if you're in HVAC — this script is yours. Hit record.

scripts.value: Extends the beloved Script This series to the trades/contractor market. Shows fear-based content works for any business category. High save/share potential from business owners in trades who feel their industry is 'unscriptable.'
scripts.cta: Find the fear. That's the content.
scripts.caption:
'HVAC is too boring to make content about.'

I wrote them a script in 60 seconds.
It got more leads than their Google Ads.

Most HVAC content: before/afters, promotions, service area.
Nobody stops for that.

The fix:
Stop posting about the business.
Start posting about the customer's fear.

Fear: 'My AC is going to die in July and I won't see it coming.'

That's the hook. That's the script.
And it books calls. 🔧

Drop your business below — I'll write yours. 👇

visuals.text: Open with slight smirk — you're proving a point, not lecturing
When delivering embedded HVAC script, shift energy — more urgent/warning tone
Text overlay during script: show 'Sign 1' and 'Sign 2' on screen as he speaks
Snap back to Shota's natural voice for BODY 3 and OUTRO

scripts.title: What I'd Do If I Started From Zero Today
scripts.hook: If I deleted my Instagram today and started completely from zero — here's exactly what I'd post for the first 30 days.
scripts.topic: Tactical 30-day content roadmap for starting from zero — proof of thinking, proof of process, one case study. Removes overwhelm, creates a clear path.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: If I deleted my Instagram today and started completely from zero — here's exactly what I'd post for the first 30 days.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Most people starting out do the same thing. They post everything at once — tips, behind the scenes, random opinions, reposted quotes. No direction. No coherent story. The account never gets traction because nobody watching can figure out: who is this person, and why should I care? So if I had to start over — one account, zero followers, no portfolio — here are the only three types of content I'd post in the first 30 days.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: This is the actual playbook—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Weeks 1 and 2: Proof of Thinking. No case studies needed. No clients needed. Just show you understand the problem better than anyone else. One post per day answering the question your ideal client is Googling at midnight. Prove your brain before you prove your results.

Week 3: Proof of Process. Show how you actually work. The framework. The system. Not the results yet — the methodology. Let them see how you think so they can imagine working with you.

Week 4: One Case Study. Doesn't have to be a massive client. Could be a friend's business you helped for free. Show the before. The decision. The after. Make it specific. Make it real.

By day 30? Twelve pieces of content that prove three things: you understand the problem, you have a system, and it actually works. That's enough to close a client.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You don't need more followers to get clients. You need better proof. Those first 30 days aren't about going viral. They're about building a body of work that earns trust with the right person when they land on your page.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: This is exactly the content system I build for every new client account at CMP. If you want it built for your business — link's in bio. And if you're starting from zero right now — save this. This is your roadmap.

scripts.value: Highest save-bait potential in batch 004. Gives any business owner a clear, specific action plan they can screenshot and follow. Removes the #1 barrier (overwhelm/not knowing where to start). Also positions CMP as the builder of this system.
scripts.cta: 30 days. 3 content types. 1 client.
scripts.caption:
If I deleted my Instagram today and started from zero — here's what I'd post for 30 days.

Week 1-2: Proof of Thinking
→ Answer the question your client Googles at midnight
→ Every. Single. Day.

Week 3: Proof of Process
→ Show your framework, your system, how you think
→ Let them imagine working with you

Week 4: One case study
→ Real. Specific. Numbers if possible.

By day 30:
12 posts. Proof you understand the problem. Proof you have a system. Proof it works.

That's enough to close a client.

Save this 🔖 — it's your roadmap.

visuals.text: Sitting down, relaxed — Build in Public, personal, like a conversation with a friend
Text overlays CRITICAL — each week's content type appears on screen as he names it
Optional: simple calendar graphic showing the 30-day breakdown
Each content type gets a clear on-screen label: 'Proof of Thinking,' 'Proof of Process,' 'Case Study'

scripts.title: Your Competitor Doesn't Post Better. They Post Smarter.
scripts.hook: Your competitor isn't posting better content than you. They're spending less time doing it. Here's the difference.
scripts.topic: Content batching — doing more with less by replacing daily scrambles with one focused weekly session.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Your competitor isn't posting better content than you. They're spending less time doing it. Here's the difference.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You're spending 2-3 hours a week staring at your phone trying to come up with something to post. One idea at a time. Day by day. And by Thursday you're completely out of juice. Meanwhile their account is consistent, clear, and always seems to have the right post at the right time. You're assuming they have a bigger team. More resources. More ideas. They don't.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: They stopped creating content one at a time. One two-hour session, once a week, produces better content than seven daily scrambles. It's called batching. You sit down with a topic list — not ideas you made up on the spot, but topics you already researched — and you script and film everything in one focused session. Five videos in two hours. Clean. Intentional. Executed. You're not winging it anymore. You're running a system. That's the entire difference between their account and yours. Not talent. Not time. Organization.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Content creation is not a daily grind. It's a weekly sprint. Treat it like a grind and you'll always be behind, always stressed, always one skipped day away from going quiet. Treat it like a sprint — one focused session, clear topic list, batch output — and you're always three posts ahead. Always.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: The first thing I build for every CMP client is a batching calendar. If you want the exact system — link's in bio. And if this clicked, follow along. This is how the whole thing works.

scripts.value: Reframes content creation from a daily grind to a weekly system. Relieves the overwhelm most business owners feel. High save potential — they want to come back to this.
scripts.cta: Batch it. Don't grind it.
scripts.caption:
Your competitor isn't posting better content than you.

They're spending less time doing it.

The difference:
You create one post at a time → always scrambling.
They batch once a week → always ahead.

One 2-hour session produces better content than 7 daily scrambles.

That's it. That's the whole thing.

Build the system. Run the sprint.
Stop grinding.

Drop a 🔖 if you needed this.

visuals.text: Open with slight exhaustion energy → shift to confident relief when revealing the system
Text overlay comparison: 'Daily scramble' ❌ vs. 'Weekly sprint' ✅
'5 videos in 2 hours' on screen as he says it — let that number land
Fast delivery, punchy — you're solving a problem they feel every week

scripts.title: I Gave a Client My Entire Script System — He Still Couldn't Post
scripts.hook: I gave a client my exact scripting system. Every template. Every framework. Every example. He came back three weeks later with zero videos posted. And it taught me the most important lesson I know about content.
scripts.topic: The gap between knowledge and execution — why systems fail when self-belief is the missing piece.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: I gave a client my exact scripting system. Every template. Every framework. Every example. He came back three weeks later with zero videos posted. And it taught me the most important lesson I know about content.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: I used to think the tools were the problem. If I just gave people the right framework — the system, the templates — they'd run with it. So I handed this client everything. He's smart. Runs a successful business. Understood the process completely. I thought this was a slam dunk. Three weeks later — nothing. Not one video filmed. Not one script finished.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: He didn't have an information problem. He had a confidence problem. Every time he sat down to film, he second-guessed the hook. Rewrote the script. Told himself it wasn't ready. The content lived in his drafts folder and never left. And I realized — the hardest part of content for most business owners isn't the scripting. It's believing that what they have to say is worth saying. They have more expertise than they realize. They just don't believe anyone wants to hear it. That's the real block. Not strategy. Not tools. Self-doubt wearing a 'strategy problem' costume.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The best system in the world doesn't work without execution. And execution starts with one belief: someone out there needs exactly what I know. You might know that intellectually. But until you feel it — until you actually believe it — the drafts folder fills up. And nothing ships.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: So — what's the piece of content you keep almost posting? Drop it in the comments. I want to know what's stuck in people's drafts right now.

scripts.value: Emotional truth-bomb that validates the hidden struggle behind content execution. Drives comments — people want to share what's in their drafts. Builds deep trust through vulnerability.
scripts.cta: Strategy without belief = a full drafts folder.
scripts.caption:
I handed a client my entire scripting system.

Templates. Framework. Examples. Everything.

Three weeks later: zero videos posted.

Here's what I learned:

The hardest part of content isn't the scripting.
It's believing what you have to say is worth saying.

That's the real block.
Self-doubt wearing a 'strategy problem' costume.

The best system in the world doesn't work without execution.
And execution starts with one belief:

Someone out there needs exactly what you know.

What's stuck in your drafts right now? Tell me. 👇

visuals.text: Walk-and-talk or casual seated — Build in Public energy, not a polished lecture
Slow down on 'He came back with zero videos' — let that land
'Drafts folder' text overlay — relatable visual anyone with a phone will recognize
Calm, slightly vulnerable delivery — this is a lesson you learned the hard way

scripts.title: Your Content Strategy Isn't Broken — You Are
scripts.hook: Your content strategy isn't broken. You are. And I say that with a lot of respect.
scripts.topic: Identity is the root cause of inconsistent content — the shift from 'I do content sometimes' to 'content IS the business.'

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Your content strategy isn't broken. You are. And I say that with a lot of respect.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You've read the threads. Watched the reels about reels. You know what you're supposed to do. Post consistently. Batch your content. Lead with a hook. You know all of it. You still don't do it. Consistency dies after week two. The system never gets implemented. The 30-day challenge gets abandoned by day four. That's not a strategy problem. That's an identity problem. You still don't see yourself as someone who makes content.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: Identity drives behavior. If deep down you believe content creation is for influencers — not business owners — you'll always do it reluctantly. Always waiting for the right moment. The right setup. The right script that's finally good enough. But the business owners who show up consistently? They've made one internal shift. They decided content is part of running their business. Not extra. Not optional. Not a favor to the algorithm. It IS the business. Once you believe that? The execution follows automatically. Not because the strategy changed. Because you changed.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: So before you buy another course or try another system — ask yourself honestly: Do I actually believe this is part of my job? Because if the answer is no, the strategy doesn't matter. The identity has to change first. Everything else is downstream of that.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: This is the conversation nobody's having in the content space. And it might be the most important one. Be honest in the comments — do you actually see yourself as a content creator? Or does that still feel like someone else's identity?

scripts.value: Hits the nerve nobody else addresses — strategy advice fails because identity is the actual blocker. High share/comment potential. Will be polarizing in the best way.
scripts.cta: Identity first. Strategy second. Always.
scripts.caption:
Hot take: your content strategy isn't broken.

You are.

(Stay with me.)

You know what to do.
Hook. Batch. Post consistently.
You still don't do it.

That's not a strategy problem.
That's an identity problem.

You don't actually see yourself as someone who makes content.

Until that changes — no strategy sticks.

The shift:
Content isn't extra.
Content IS the business.

Once you believe that? The consistency follows automatically.

Identity first. Strategy second.

Be honest: do you see yourself as a content creator?
Or does that still feel like someone else's thing? ⬇️

visuals.text: Lean in slightly — direct, calm, not aggressive. You're diagnosing, not attacking.
Text overlay at hook: 'Your strategy isn't broken. You are.' — bold, centered, hold for 2 seconds
When listing what they 'know but don't do' — fast, staccato delivery
Slow down on 'Identity drives behavior' — this is the thesis

scripts.title: Script This: Dentist
scripts.hook: Dentists think they can't make emotional content because dentistry is too clinical. I wrote a dental practice a script that doubled their new patient consultations.
scripts.topic: Scripting a dental practice using the fear-reversal format — addressing patient shame/anxiety before it prevents the booking.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Dentists think they can't make emotional content because dentistry is too clinical. I wrote a dental practice a script that doubled their new patient consultations.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what dental content looks like right now. Straight-teeth before-and-afters. 'We now offer Invisalign.' Some dentist explaining a root canal with a model tooth and a lot of pointing. Nobody stops for any of that. Nobody shares it. Nobody calls because of it. Because it talks about the service — not the person sitting in a waiting room trying not to have a panic attack.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the script—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: '57% of people who need dental work are putting it off right now — because they're scared of the pain, the judgment, or the bill. This one's for them.'

Body: 'If you haven't been to the dentist in two years or more — first of all, you're not alone. Our most common new patient? Someone who waited too long, knows it, and is embarrassed to walk in. Here's what I want you to know: we've seen everything. We don't judge. We fix it. And 90% of the time? It is not as bad as the version you've built up in your head. Book the appointment you've been avoiding. We'll handle the rest.'

Then you show a real patient testimonial. That's the whole video.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not selling cleanings and fillings. You're selling relief from the shame and fear that's kept someone out of your chair for two years. Name the thing they're too embarrassed to say out loud — and the phone rings. Every dentist in America has a waiting room full of patients who almost didn't come. That's your content.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you run a dental practice and want a content system built around new patient acquisition — link's in bio. That's exactly what CMP does. And drop your practice type in the comments. You might be next.

scripts.value: Expands Script This series to healthcare/dental — a massive untapped category. Proves emotional content works in 'boring' clinical industries. High save potential from dentists + healthcare providers.
scripts.cta: Name the fear. Fill the chair.
scripts.caption:
Dentists think they can't make emotional content.

I wrote one a script that doubled their new patient consults.

Most dental content: before/afters, 'we offer Invisalign,' a dentist pointing at a tooth model.

Nobody stops for that.

Here's what works:

57% of people who need dental work are avoiding it.
Scared of the pain. The judgment. The bill.

Name that fear in your content.
Give them permission to come in anyway.

That's the video that gets shared.
That's the video that books patients.

Drop your practice type below 👇 — you might be next.

visuals.text: When delivering the embedded dental script, shift energy — slower, warmer, more reassuring tone
Text overlay during embedded script: '57% are avoiding it' on screen as he says it
Snap back to confident Shota for Body 3 — the contrast between patient-facing script and business lesson
Clean, direct setup — no distracting background elements

scripts.title: The DM That Closed the Deal
scripts.hook: Most people don't know what to say when someone DMs them after a video. So the conversation dies. Here's the 3-message sequence that turns 'I love your content' into a paid client.

scripts.title: Script This: Chiropractor
scripts.hook: Chiropractors have some of the most powerful content potential of any business — and almost none of them are using it. I wrote one a script that books new patients without a single ad dollar spent.

scripts.title: I Raised My Prices and Got More Clients
scripts.hook: I raised my prices by 40% last year. And then I signed more clients in the following 60 days than I had in the 6 months before that. Here's what actually happened.

scripts.title: The Content ROI Nobody Tracks
scripts.hook: Every business owner tracking their content ROI is measuring the wrong thing. And their social media manager is letting them.

scripts.title: Your Niche Isn't Too Small — It's Too Vague
scripts.hook: Your niche isn't too small. It's too vague. And that's why the right people are watching your content and not calling you.

scripts.title: The 3 Videos Every Service Business Needs Before Any Other Content
scripts.hook: Before you post another tip, another reel, another behind-the-scenes — your service business needs these 3 videos. Nothing else matters until these exist.
scripts.topic: Minimum viable content library — the three foundational videos that build a trust funnel for any service business.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Before you post another tip, another reel, another behind-the-scenes — your service business needs these 3 videos. Nothing else matters until these exist.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Most service businesses post content in random order. A tip one day. A testimonial the next. Some behind-the-scenes. A holiday post. None of it connects. None of it builds toward anything. Someone lands on your page for the first time and can't figure out in five seconds who you help, what you do, or why they should care. That's not a content problem. That's a foundation problem. You're building walls without a floor.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the foundation—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Video one: The Problem Video. One post that names your ideal client's number one pain with so much precision that they stop scrolling and think — 'they're talking about me.' This is how you pull the right person in.

Video two: The Proof Video. One post showing a real result. Before, the decision, the after. Real story. Real numbers if you have them. This is your credibility.

Video three: The Process Video. One post explaining how you work. What makes your approach different. Why it actually works. This is your differentiator.

Those three videos — when they exist — turn a profile visitor into someone who DMs you. Everything else is amplification. Tips, reels, behind-the-scenes — they all build on top of these three. Without the foundation, the rest is noise.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You don't need 50 posts. You need 3 that do 3 jobs: pull the right person in, prove you can help them, and show them exactly how. Build the floor first. Then stack everything else on top. That's the system.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you want help building these three foundational videos for your business — that's where every single CMP client starts. Link's in bio. And comment below — which of these three are you missing right now?

scripts.value: Highest save-bait in batch 005. Gives any service business owner a clear, immediately actionable framework. Removes overwhelm. Screenshot-worthy list. Positions CMP as the builder of this foundation.
scripts.cta: Problem + Proof + Process = The Foundation.
scripts.caption:
Before you post another tip or reel —

Your service business needs these 3 videos first.

1️⃣ The Problem Video
Names your client's #1 pain with precision.
Pulls the right person in.

2️⃣ The Proof Video
Real result. Real story. Real numbers.
Builds credibility before they DM you.

3️⃣ The Process Video
How you work. What makes you different. Why it works.
Gives them a reason to choose YOU.

Those 3 → profile visitor becomes a DM.
Everything else is amplification on top of that foundation.

Which one are you missing right now?
Drop it below 👇 — I read every comment.
